@@ -146,13 +146,13 @@ {"id":"bd-2x0.9","title":"Implement Markdown tokenizer","description":"# Task: Implement Markdown tokenizer ## Context Markdown is the de facto standard for documentation, READMEs, and notes. Terminal-based editors and tools frequently work with Markdown, making it a high-value tokenizer to include. ## Markdown Syntax Elements ### Headings ```markdown # Heading 1 ## Heading 2 ### Heading 3 (up to 6) Alternative Heading ================== Alternative H2 -------------- ``` ### Emphasis ```markdown *italic* or _italic_ **bold** or __bold__ ***bold italic*** ~~strikethrough~~ ``` ### Code ```markdown `inline code` indented code block (4 spaces) ```language fenced code block ``` ``` ### Links and Images ```markdown [link text](url) [link text](url "title") [reference link][ref] [ref]: url ![alt text](image.png) ![alt text](image.png "title") ``` ### Lists ```markdown - Unordered item * Also unordered + Also unordered 1. Ordered item 2. Ordered item - [ ] Task item unchecked - [x] Task item checked ``` ### Blockquotes ```markdown > Quote line > Continued quote ``` ### Horizontal Rules ```markdown --- *** ___ ``` ### Tables (GFM) ```markdown | Header | Header | |--------|--------| | Cell | Cell | ``` ### HTML ```markdown
Raw HTML
``` ## Implementation Create `src/highlight/languages/markdown.rs`: ```rust pub struct MarkdownTokenizer; impl Tokenizer for MarkdownTokenizer { fn name(&self) -> &str { "Markdown" } fn extensions(&self) -> &[&str] { &["md", "markdown", "mkd", "mkdn"] } fn tokenize_line(&self, line: &str, state: LineState) -> (Vec, LineState) { // Handle fenced code block continuation // Line-start elements: headings, lists, blockquotes, HR // Inline elements: emphasis, code, links } } ``` ### Token Mapping | Markdown Element | TokenKind | |-----------------|-----------| | `# Heading` | Heading | | `*italic*` | Emphasis | | `**bold**` | Emphasis + Bold style | | `[text](url)` | Link | | `![alt](url)` | Link | | `` `code` `` | CodeInline | | ``` ```code``` ``` | CodeBlock | | `> quote` | Comment (or custom Quote) | | `- item` | Punctuation + Text | | `---` | Punctuation | ## Multi-line Handling 1. **Fenced code blocks** - ``` ... ``` (track the fence marker) 2. **Indented code blocks** - 4+ spaces at line start 3. **Multi-line blockquotes** - Lines starting with `>` ## Edge Cases - Escaping: `\*not italic\*` - Nested emphasis: `***bold and italic***` - Links with parentheses in URL - Code spans with backticks inside: ``` `` `code` `` ``` - Setext headings (underline style) - HTML blocks ## Dependencies - Requires: bd-2x0.2 (Tokenizer trait) ## Testing ```rust #[test] fn test_md_headings() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_emphasis() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_code_inline() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_code_blocks() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_links() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_lists() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_blockquotes() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_escaping() { ... } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - CREATE: `src/highlight/languages/markdown.rs` - MODIFY: `src/highlight/languages/mod.rs` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Headings (ATX and Setext styles) - [ ] Emphasis (italic, bold, strikethrough) - [ ] Inline and fenced code - [ ] Links and images - [ ] Lists (ordered, unordered, task) - [ ] Blockquotes - [ ] Fenced code block continuation - [ ] Escape sequences - [ ] Test coverage >85%","notes":"AGENTS.md alignment: Performance target <1μs/line. Zero-allocation tokenization. Tests must use tracing (info!, debug!) for detailed logging. Markdown is high-value for documentation editing in terminal apps.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:46:23.879677476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T12:19:02.704966007Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T12:19:02.704947462Z","close_reason":"Implemented Markdown tokenizer + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["highlight","lang"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"}]} {"id":"bd-2xk1","title":"demo_showcase quality: run fmt/clippy/test gates before landing","description":"# demo_showcase Quality — Run fmt/clippy/test Gates Before Landing ## Purpose The demo will touch many parts of the codebase. This bead ensures we don’t “ship a broken flagship”. ## Required Gates Run these locally (and ensure CI passes): - `cargo fmt --check` - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` - `cargo test` If demo modifies hot paths or rendering behavior, optionally run the buffer benchmark suite (`cargo bench --bench buffer`) as a sanity check. ## Acceptance Notes - No formatting drift. - No clippy warnings. - All tests pass, including demo compile/smoke tests.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` all pass after demo_showcase implementation. - demo_showcase-specific suites are included: compile coverage, headless smoke, JSON snapshots, tour determinism, degradation matrix, and core headless checks. - Optional PTY E2E tests remain `#[ignore]` but are runnable via scripts and provide actionable artifacts when enabled.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.666703286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:08.399425192Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-10de","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:50.821280848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-1n67","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:51.409960028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2649","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:18.505590317Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:10.570568702Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-31gz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:52.010278354Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.679232573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-3cj0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:20.439486754Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-bqd1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.058244445Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-jfno","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.654855091Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-313","title":"Expand benchmark coverage beyond buffer module","description":"Currently only benches/buffer.rs exists. Add benchmarks for: renderer diff detection, color blending operations, text buffer operations, unicode width calculations.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:46.184813379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:37:44.657047735Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:37:44.656970329Z","close_reason":"Comprehensive benchmarks added for color blending (creation, blending, conversion, interpolation), text buffer (TextBuffer, EditBuffer, cursor movement, undo/redo), renderer diff detection (identical, single change, row change, scattered, full diff), and unicode width calculations (ASCII, CJK, emoji, grapheme operations). All benchmarks compile and run successfully.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} -{"id":"bd-31gz","title":"demo_showcase docs: README section (how to run + flags + keybinds)","description":"# demo_showcase Docs — README Section (How to Run + Flags + Keybinds) ## Purpose The flagship demo should be discoverable. Add a README section that explains: - how to run the demo - what it demonstrates - essential keybinds - tour mode and headless smoke mode - recommended terminals for best visuals ## Content Requirements Add a `Demo Showcase` section to `README.md`: Run: - cargo run --bin demo_showcase Tour (unattended): - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour --exit-after-tour Headless smoke: - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke Headless JSON dump (for tests/debugging): - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json Degradation matrix (example manual run): - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json --headless-size 50x15 --cap-preset minimal Document keybinds (or point to the in-app help overlay) and call out terminal recommendations (kitty/alacritty/wezterm/ghostty) for best results. ## Flag Summary (Must Match bd-2iv) Include a small, explicit list of supported flags (grouped by category): Interactive: - --fps - --tour - --exit-after-tour - --max-frames Headless/testing: - --headless-smoke - --headless-dump-json - --headless-check - --headless-size Terminal behavior: - --no-mouse - --no-alt-screen - --no-cap-queries Capability override: - --cap-preset Advanced: - --threaded - --seed ## Acceptance Notes - README makes it obvious the demo exists and how to run it. - Flags documented match the actual CLI (bd-2iv).","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `README.md` contains a clear `Demo Showcase` section with run commands (interactive, tour, headless). - The documented flag list matches the implemented CLI (`bd-2iv`) including headless size + capability preset controls. - The README points users to the in-app help overlay for the authoritative keybind list.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.288336317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:14.485670643Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-1gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:38.026379403Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:36.008108527Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.300717928Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:37.034595590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"bd-31gz","title":"demo_showcase docs: README section (how to run + flags + keybinds)","description":"# demo_showcase Docs — README Section (How to Run + Flags + Keybinds) ## Purpose The flagship demo should be discoverable. The README points users to the in-app help overlay for the authoritative keybind list.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.288336317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:59:51.498373446Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:59:51.498306461Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-1gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:38.026379403Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:36.008108527Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.300717928Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:37.034595590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-35e","title":"demo_showcase UI: toast + notification system (stack + fade)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Toast + Notification System (Stack + Fade) ## Purpose Toasts make the demo feel like a real application and also give us a constant excuse to show: - translucent overlays (opacity stack) - animated alpha fades (time-driven) - crisp typography + spacing They also provide immediate feedback when commands/actions occur. ## Behaviors - Toasts appear in a consistent corner (e.g., bottom-right above the status bar). - Multiple toasts stack with a small vertical gap. - Each toast has: - severity: Info / Warn / Error - title + optional detail line - TTL (auto-dismiss) with fade-out - optional “action” hint (e.g., `R` retry) ## Rendering Notes - Background should be semi-transparent with a gradient edge or subtle border. - Severity affects accent color and icon glyph. - Clip the toast region with scissor so it never draws into the status bar. ## Integration Points Toasts should be produced by: - command palette executions - tour step transitions - simulated preview errors/retries - clipboard/link actions Implementation suggestion: - `struct Toast { created_at, ttl, level, title, detail, ... }` - `struct ToastManager { toasts: VecDeque }` - render in the main render pass after panels but before full-screen overlays.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - Toasts render as translucent cards with consistent spacing, typography, and severity styling. - Toasts stack with a gap and auto-dismiss after TTL with a smooth fade. - Toast region is clipped so it never draws into the status bar. - Core actions (palette commands, tour steps, paste, resize, simulated preview error) generate toasts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:23.894150944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:16:13.720258754Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:16:13.720185457Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-1pd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:00.676575823Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-2t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:01.203719095Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:23.906489096Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:00.094359519Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-35g","title":"demo_showcase runtime: App state machine (modes, focus, event routing)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — App State Machine (Modes, Focus, Routing) ## Purpose Define a clean “brain” for the demo so the UI remains coherent as features accumulate. ## Core State Suggested structure: ```rust enum Mode { Normal, Help, CommandPalette, Tour, } enum Focus { Sidebar, Editor, Preview, Logs, } enum Section { Overview, Editor, Preview, Logs, Unicode, Performance, Settings, } struct App { mode: Mode, focus: Focus, section: Section, paused: bool, // e.g. focus lost // Panel state sidebar: SidebarState, editor: EditorState, preview: PreviewState, logs: LogState, // Overlay state palette: PaletteState, help: HelpState, tour: TourState, // UX polish toasts: ToastQueue, last_resize: Option<(u32,u32)>, } ``` ## Section vs Focus (Important) - **Focus** answers: “Where do key presses go?” - **Section** answers: “What content is the app showing / emphasizing?” In wide layouts, all panels remain visible; `section` drives preview content (pixels vs unicode vs perf) and sets the default focus when navigating. In compact layouts, `section` selects the primary full-screen panel. ## Event Routing Rules 1. **Global shortcuts first** (quit/help/palette/tour/debug/invalidate) 2. **System events next** (resize, focus gained/lost) 3. **Mode-specific** handling (help/palette/tour) 4. **Focused panel** receives remaining key/mouse events This prevents conflicts and keeps behavior predictable. ## Action Layer (Optional but Recommended) Instead of mutating state directly in input handlers, parse events into actions: ```rust enum Action { Quit, ToggleHelp, TogglePalette, ToggleTour, CycleFocus(i32), NavigateSection(Section), Editor(EditorAction), Sidebar(SidebarAction), Preview(PreviewAction), Logs(LogsAction), Resize(u32,u32), FocusChanged(bool), } ``` This makes tour mode trivial: it just emits Actions. ## Focus / Hit Testing Integration Mouse clicks should: - map screen coords → HitGrid id - translate id → semantic action (e.g. “sidebar item 3”) - update focus + selection consistently ## Output A well-defined state machine that later UI beads can build on without “spaghetti input handling”.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist: - [ ] Modes are explicit (Normal/Help/Palette/Tour) - [ ] Focus routing is explicit and consistent - [ ] Global shortcuts take precedence and never conflict - [ ] Resize and focus events update app state predictably - [ ] HitGrid click IDs map cleanly to semantic actions - [ ] Tour mode can drive the app via the same action pathway","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:36.517144360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:00:55.521724803Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:00:55.521591955Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35g","depends_on_id":"bd-1m9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:59.454327871Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35g","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:36.530331471Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35g","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:56.645724337Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35g","depends_on_id":"bd-2mu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:54.177006201Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35g","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:51.610250289Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-35rk","title":"demo_showcase features: HitGrid + mouse interactions (clickable UI + hover/pressed)","description":"# demo_showcase Features — HitGrid + Mouse Interactions (Clickable UI + Hover/Pressed) ## Purpose A “turned up to 11” demo should be *clickable*. OpenTUI’s `HitGrid` is how we map mouse coordinates to UI actions. This bead ensures mouse interaction feels real and demonstrates: - hit testing correctness - hit scissoring correctness (no clicks through clipped regions) - keyboard/mouse parity (same actions available) ## Hit ID Scheme Define a stable mapping from hit IDs → actions. Example approach (demo-only): - Reserve ID ranges by component: - `1000-1999` chrome buttons - `2000-2999` sidebar rows - `3000-3999` editor area - `4000-4999` preview toggles - `5000-5999` log rows - `9000+` overlay controls The exact numbers aren’t important; stability and debuggability are. ## Rendering Requirements - Every interactive element registers hit areas during render: - `Renderer::register_hit_area(x, y, w, h, id)` - Use `Renderer::push_hit_scissor(rect)` around clipped lists so hit areas never extend beyond visible rows. ## Interaction Requirements - Click to focus panels. - Click sidebar item to switch section. - Click log row to select. - Click overlay buttons (close/next/pause). Optional polish: - Hover styling (if mouse move events are available): subtly highlight hovered row. - Pressed styling: show a brief “pressed” state on click. ## Acceptance Notes - No hit areas fire outside the visible scissor regions. - Mouse interactions work even when the UI is resizing.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - A stable hit-id scheme is defined (reserved ranges per component) and used consistently. - Click interactions work for: top bar buttons, sidebar rows, overlay controls, and selectable log rows. - Hit scissoring is correct: clipped regions cannot be clicked outside the visible area. - Visual feedback exists for hover (when available) and pressed states (on click). - Keyboard/mouse parity holds: every click action has an equivalent keyboard path. - When mouse is disabled (`--no-mouse` or equivalent gating), the UI remains fully operable via keyboard and does not show misleading click hints.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:27:55.640771011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:37:20.527958377Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:37:20.527890260Z","close_reason":"Implemented HitGrid mouse interactions: hit ID constants, panel focus click, sidebar section click, command palette item click. Hit areas registered in render pass with scissor-aware bounds. Keyboard/mouse parity achieved for core navigation.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-1al","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:27.303690270Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-1e4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:26.192658052Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-1fu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:26.754711998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-1m9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:25.037470993Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:25.595167417Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-37p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:27:55.656569873Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:17.024063982Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-37p","title":"[EPIC] demo_showcase: advanced features prove-out (graphemes, hitgrid, hyperlinks, pixels)","description":"# Epic: demo_showcase — Advanced Feature Prove‑Out ## Purpose Ensure the demo app explicitly and recognizably exercises OpenTUI’s “wow” features: - Grapheme pool rendering (multi-codepoint emoji / ZWJ sequences) - Hyperlinks via OSC 8 - Hit testing grid + mouse interactions - Pixel-level rendering primitives (supersampling / grayscale) - Scissor + opacity stacks in nested UI contexts ## Key Principle These features must not appear as isolated “hello world” snippets. They should be integrated into believable UI behaviors (preview panels, clickable controls, tooltips, etc.).","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist: - [ ] Grapheme pool is exercised with a visible “this would break without pool” string - [ ] Hyperlink cells are rendered and clickable in supported terminals - [ ] Mouse click/scroll drives real navigation via `HitGrid` - [ ] PixelBuffer/GrayscaleBuffer output is integrated into the UI, not a separate screen - [ ] At least one overlay uses opacity stack + blending for a glass effect","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:34.519122126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:44.123727790Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","epic","features"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37p","depends_on_id":"bd-1co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:34.541866115Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-383","title":"[EPIC] demo_showcase: tests, docs, and quality gates","description":"# Epic: demo_showcase — Tests, Docs, Quality Gates ## Purpose A flagship demo that breaks silently is worse than no demo. This epic ensures we have layered confidence: - fast compile coverage - deterministic headless runtime checks - snapshot regressions for UI/feature markers - optional true E2E PTY tests for ANSI/terminal behavior - scripts to run and collect artifacts - documentation so the demo is discoverable ## Deliverables (This Epic Owns) ### CI-Friendly - Compile coverage for the new binary target - Headless smoke mode + test that runs it - Snapshot regression suite (insta) based on headless JSON output - Tour determinism regression suite (headless tour + JSON) ### Local / Developer Workflows - Optional PTY-based E2E tests (ignored by default) that assert on ANSI sequences - One-command scripts in `scripts/` to run checks and collect artifacts ### Documentation - README: how to run, flags, tour mode, headless smoke - In-app help overlay is the primary keybind reference ## Constraints - Tests must be deterministic and CI-friendly. - Avoid fragile “golden screenshot” tests that depend on terminal quirks. - When tests fail, logs must be actionable (include reproduction commands + stored artifacts).","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist: - [ ] `cargo test` includes a compile test for `demo_showcase` - [ ] Headless smoke tests validate core layout/render functions - [ ] README includes how to run the demo + what it proves - [ ] In-app help overlay documents keybinds and features - This bead adds one or more scripts in `scripts/` that: - run headless smoke + JSON dump - run snapshot tests (including degradation matrix) - optionally run ignored PTY tests - collect stdout/stderr and captured ANSI bytes into an artifacts directory ## Proposed Script(s) ### 1) scripts/demo_showcase_verify.sh A fast, CI-like validation pass: - cargo fmt --check - cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo test Then (optional but useful for quick manual inspection): - cargo run --all-features --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json --max-frames 10 ### 2) scripts/demo_showcase_e2e_pty.sh Runs ignored PTY tests locally with artifacts enabled: - HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1 HARNESS_ARTIFACTS_DIR=target/test-artifacts \ cargo test --test demo_showcase_pty_e2e -- --ignored --nocapture ## Artifact Conventions Reuse the existing harness conventions: - HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1 - HARNESS_ARTIFACTS_DIR=target/test-artifacts Artifacts should include: - headless JSON dumps - snapshot mismatch output (if any) - degradation matrix JSON snapshots (bd-2bnv) - PTY captured ANSI bytes (raw + escaped/hex) when PTY tests run ## Acceptance Notes - Running the verify script gives a clear PASS/FAIL summary. - On failure, it prints reproduction commands and points to artifact paths.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `scripts/demo_showcase_verify.sh` runs fmt + clippy + tests and returns non-zero on failure. - used saturating_mul for integer overflow prevention and added 10MB limit on paste buffer","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-3ii","title":"demo_showcase runtime: layout helpers (Rect, splitting, clamping, compact mode)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Layout Helpers ## Purpose OpenTUI intentionally does not ship a layout system. The demo still needs *readable, reusable* layout helpers. ## Minimal Layout Toolkit (Inside the Binary) Define a tiny `Rect` and helpers: ```rust #[derive(Clone, Copy)] struct Rect { x: i32, y: i32, w: u32, h: u32 } impl Rect { fn inset(self, pad: u32) -> Rect { ... } fn split_h(self, left_w: u32) -> (Rect, Rect) { ... } fn split_v(self, top_h: u32) -> (Rect, Rect) { ... } fn clamp_to(self, w: u32, h: u32) -> Rect { ... } } ``` Also provide layout decisions: - sidebar width (min/max) - preview width - status bar height - top bar height ## Compact Layout Mode When terminal is too small, we switch to a single-panel layout: - Show top bar + status bar - Main content = focused panel full-screen - Sidebar becomes an overlay or toggled view This implements the resilience rules from `bd-1i7`. ## Scissor Strategy For each panel: - compute outer rect (border) - compute inner rect (content) - `buffer.push_scissor(inner_clip)` - draw content - `buffer.pop_scissor()` ## Output A tiny, deterministic layout layer that keeps the demo’s code maintainable.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist: - [ ] `Rect` + split/inset helpers exist (minimal but sufficient) - [ ] Panel rectangles are computed in one place per frame - [ ] Compact layout mode rules are implemented - [ ] Scissor usage is standardized per panel","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:00.689913873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:33:10.039456325Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:33:10.039326443Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ii","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:06.640056476Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ii","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:00.719418675Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ii","depends_on_id":"bd-2mu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:01.973808970Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-3iv","title":"Add token bounds validation in highlighted_buffer","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:54:14.063935824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:00:06.166612370Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:00:06.166550585Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
